source.title: Customer Health Audit & Risk Mitigation Playbook
source.author: Lina Park
source.content:
Summary

This playbook describes the Customer Health Audit process used by Redwood Customer Success to surface operational, cost, and quality risks for enterprise accounts running Redwood Hosted, Dedicated, or Private deployments. The goal is to provide a repeatable audit cadence, a quantitative risk score, concrete remediation actions, and an executive-ready summary for QBRs and escalations.

Scope

- Applies to enterprise and dedicated customers with production traffic > 10k API calls/day or with a Dedicated/Private contract.
- Covers three domains: Service Reliability (latency/errors), Model Quality (regression & hallucination signals), and Economics (cost per 1k tokens / spend trending).
- Audit cadence: monthly for at-risk customers, quarterly for healthy customers, and ad-hoc after major incidents or rollouts.

Roles & Ownership

- Customer Success Manager (CSM): overall owner of the audit, primary contact for the customer, coordinates remediation work.
- Solutions Engineer (SE): operational lead; runs diagnostics, implements configuration changes (batching, cache), and validates fixes.
- Product/Applied ML: consulted when pattern suggests model/quality regression or recommended model changes.
- SRE/Support: handles low-level runtime issues, capacity, or infra incidents.

Audit Preparation (2–3 business days before assessment)

1. Trigger: CSM schedules audit in calendar and notifies SE and customer with scope and data window (last 30 days by default).
2. Data collection checklist (attach to audit ticket):
   - Logs: request/response traces, latency histograms (p50/p90/p99), error logs (last 30 days).
   - Billing: per-route token breakdown, monthly spend, top 10 routes by cost.
   - Model settings: pinned model versions, quantization profile, batch/timeout configs.
   - Prompts and evals: recent prompt sets, failing prompts flagged by QA.
   - Customer config: routing policies, region preferences, cache settings.

Audit Checklist (executed by SE + CSM)

- Service Reliability
  - Median latency per route (p50): check against SLO. Thresholds: p50 > 250ms (warning), p50 > 500ms (critical).
  - Tail latency (p99): p99 > 1.5s (warning), p99 > 3s (critical).
  - Error rate: total 5xx > 0.5% (warning), > 2% (critical).
  - Throttling/rate-limit events: frequency and root cause (customer spikes vs infra).

- Model Quality
  - Regression alerts: responses failing customer gold prompts increased by >10% vs baseline.
  - Hallucination signals: manual QA or automated semantic checks flagged >3% of sampled responses.
  - Token truncation/early-eos events: >1% of responses truncated unexpectedly.

- Economics
  - Cost-per-1k tokens: compare current month vs prior 3-month average. Delta > +20% => warning; > +50% => critical.
  - Inefficient routes: top 5 routes by cost and token-per-call ratios.
  - Batching utilization: low utilization on high-throughput routes indicates missed savings.

Risk Scoring Rubric

Assign numeric points per observed item then compute a weighted score (0–100). Categories and weights: Reliability 40, Quality 35, Economics 25.

- Example point assignments (single audit window):
  - Reliability: critical p99 events (+30), repeated 5xx spikes (+20), throttling blocking production (+25).
  - Quality: regression alert cluster (+20), hallucination >5% (+25), degradation on Canary vs Prod (+15).
  - Economics: sudden spend delta +50% (+20), misconfigured batching (+10), cache disabled (+15).

Total Score Interpretation:
- 0–29: Healthy. Routine follow-up.
- 30–59: At-risk. Action items required within 14 days.
- 60–100: High risk. Escalation recommended; consider canary rollback or temporary throttling.

Example Audit Table (for insertion into ticket)

| Domain | Metric | Observed | Threshold | Severity |
|-------|--------|----------|-----------|----------|
| Reliability | p99 latency (route /generate) | 2.8s | 1.5s (warn) / 3s (crit) | warning |
| Quality | Gold prompt pass rate | 78% (down from 92%) | 90% baseline | critical |
| Economics | Cost / 1k tokens (monthly) | $18.20 | +20% warning | warning |

Immediate Remediations (actions to perform in the first 72 hours)

1. If Quality critical: rollback model variant or pin prior working model; open applied-ml ticket for evaluation. Steps:
   a. Identify offending model deployment and associated rollout ID.
   b. Execute canary rollback: follow internal runbook (link: /eng-developer-experience/api-examples/canary-rollback).
   c. Notify customer and schedule investigation call.
2. If Reliability critical: apply temporary rate-limiter or route traffic to fallback region/model. Steps:
   a. Enable emergency throttles on affected routes (SE + SRE).
   b. Re-route high-cost/latency traffic to a cheaper lower-latency region or a smaller model variant with comparable quality.
3. If Economics critical: enable batching and prefix/KV caching where safe; review quantization profile. Steps:
   a. Suggest config changes: increase batch_size cap, set adaptive batching to true, enable KV prefix cache for repeated prompts.
   b. Validate token accounting and project expected cost delta.

Escalation Flow & Triage Matrix

- At-risk (score 30–59): CSM + SE action within 14 days, weekly check-ins. If unresolved after 2 cycles escalate to Customer Success Lead.
- High risk (score 60+): Immediate cross-functional war room: CSM, SE, SRE, Product, Applied ML; timeline goals: initial mitigation within 24 hours, full remediation plan within 72 hours.
- Executive escalation: prepare a 1-page exec summary; notify account executive and VP Customer Success for renewal-impacting accounts.

Communication Templates

- Customer initial notice (CSM to customer):
  Subject: Customer Health Audit — Findings & Immediate Actions
  Body: Short summary (2–3 bullets), impact statement, immediate mitigations applied, next steps and timeline,war-room contact.

- Internal war-room kickoff (CSM to cross-functional list):
  Subject: WAR ROOM — [Account] — High Risk Audit (score: XX)
  Body: One-line TL;DR, key metrics, links to dashboards, owner assignments, meeting link.

Quarterly Executive Summary Template (for QBR slide deck)

- Page title: Operational Health & Trends — [Customer Name] — Last 90 days
- TL;DR: one-sentence health band (Healthy / At-risk / High risk) and recommended executive ask (e.g., budget approval, architecture change).
- Key metrics: uptime %, p99 latency, monthly spend trend, model pass-rate on gold prompts.
- Top incidents and mitigations: list 3–5 items with dates and impact.
- Action plan & commitments: items, owners, due dates.
- Ask of the customer: engineering time, dataset for evals, or approval to change model variant.

Audit Ticketing & Tracking

- Create a case in Zendesk (or internal tracker) with prefix CHA-[account]-YYYYMM. Include: audit score, checklist, remediation tasks (as subtasks), and due dates.
- Use label: health-audit and priority tags (P2 for at-risk, P1 for high risk).
- Link ticket to the customer’s support backlog and to the QBR board for follow-up.

Post-Audit Follow-ups (14–90 days)

- 14-day: verify mitigations are effective, update customer and close immediate fixes.
- 30-day: confirm metric regressions reversed and any config rollouts stabilized.
- 90-day: re-run audit cadence, update QBR materials, and assess if SLA amendments are needed.

Instrumentation & Dashboards

- Required dashboards and saved queries (examples):
  - Per-route latency: p50/p90/p99 and traffic volume.
  - Model pass-rate on gold prompt set (rolling 7d/30d).
  - Cost rollup by route and model variant (daily).
  - Batching/caching utilization by service.

Example SQL snippet (monthly cost by route)

SELECT route, SUM(tokens_in + tokens_out) AS total_tokens, SUM(cost) AS total_cost
FROM billing.events
WHERE account_id = '<<ACCOUNT_ID>>' AND event_date >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY)
GROUP BY route
ORDER BY total_cost DESC
LIMIT 20;

Appendix: Common Findings & Recommended Fixes

- Large spikes in p99 tied to specific third-party integrations: recommend isolating those flows and adding retry/backoff.
- Quality dip after model refresh: recommend immediate canary rollback + larger regression evaluation on labelled prompts.
- Unexpected token spend: typically caused by template expansion loops or verbose system prompts — recommend prompt audit and token budget caps per route.

References & Links

- Support runbooks: ../support-process/enterprise-support-lifecycle-dedicated-private-playbook-2025
- Canary rollback runbook: ../eng-developer-experience/docs/canary-rollback-guide
- Model evaluation harness: ../applied-ml-and-evals/eval-harness/overview

Change log

- 2025-02-10: Created by Lina Park (initial framework).
- 2025-10-05: Added scoring rubric and immediate remediation templates (review: Marcus Liu).
- 2026-01-18: Updated thresholds and SQL sample; clarified escalation timelines (review: Priya Natarajan).
